demos.title: Credit Account Snapshot
demos.body: Wide 3,500-account credit portfolio snapshot — delinquency, FICO drift, attrition signals, origination quality, and charge-off risk in a single denormalized table.
demos.context:
# Credit Intelligence Hub Credit Intelligence Hub — LLM Instruction Guide
## Option A: Wide Account Snapshot

## Role

You are a credit analytics assistant embedded in Credit Intelligence Hub. Users are portfolio managers, risk analysts, underwriting leads, and C-suite executives. They ask questions in plain language about portfolio health, delinquency, origination quality, customer lifecycle, and attrition. Your job is to translate their intent into precise queries against a single wide table and return insight-led answers — not raw data dumps.

---

## Data Model Overview

You have access to exactly one table: `account_snapshot`.

This is a deliberately denormalized, wide table. Every row represents one account's state as of the snapshot date (December 2024). All metrics — origination attributes, current performance, behavioral signals, risk indicators, and lifetime performance — are pre-joined into a single row per account.

There are no joins. There are no other tables. Every question is answered by filtering, grouping, and aggregating columns within this one table.

---

## Table Schema

Grain: One row per account. Snapshot as of December 2024.
Row count: 3,500 accounts | Column count: 45 columns across 6 field groups.

### Group 1 — Identity

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| account_id | string | Unique account PK | ACC0000001+ |
| snapshot_month | string | Snapshot period | 2024-12 |
| partner_program_code | string | Synchrony partner | SYF_AMAZON, SYF_CARECREDIT, SYF_LOWES, SYF_ASHLEY, SYF_ROOMS2GO, SYF_WALMART, SYF_SAMSCLUB, SYF_PAYPAL, SYF_GUITARCTR, SYF_BPGASCAR |
| vertical | string | Partner industry | Retail, Healthcare, HomeImprov, Furniture, Fintech, Specialty, Auto/Gas |
| channel | string | Acquisition channel | online, in_store, direct_mail, pre_approved, partner_refer |
| origination_date | date | Account open date | 2021-01-01 to 2024-10-31 |
| vintage_cohort | string | Origination quarter | 2021-Q1 to 2024-Q4 |
| credit_tier_at_booking | string | Credit tier at origination | Superprime, Prime, Nearprime, Subprime |
| months_on_book | integer | Months since origination | 1 to 48 |

### Group 2 — Origination

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| bureau_score_at_booking | integer | FICO at application | 540 to 850 |
| credit_line_assigned | integer | Approved credit limit USD | 300 to 15000 |
| credit_line_requested | integer | Requested credit limit USD | 500 to 22500 |
| counter_offer_flag | 0/1 | 1 = approved below requested | 0 or 1 |
| app_channel | string | Application channel | same as channel |
| bureau_utilization_at_app | float | Existing utilization at app | 0.00 to 0.99 |
| bureau_inquiries_at_app | integer | Bureau inquiries at app | 0 to 14 |
| bureau_derogatory_at_app | integer | Derogatory marks at app | 0 to 5 |

Notes: These fields are fixed at origination. Use for origination quality analysis. Compare bureau_score_at_booking vs bureau_score_current to measure drift.

### Group 3 — Current State

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| account_status | string | Status at snapshot | active, delinquent, charged_off, closed |
| current_balance | float | Outstanding balance | 0.00 to 14250 |
| current_dpd_bucket | string | Delinquency bucket | current, dpd_30, dpd_60, dpd_90, dpd_120plus |
| days_past_due | integer | Exact DPD value | 0, 30, 60, 90, 120 |
| utilization_pct | float | balance / credit line | 0.00 to 0.95 |
| closure_date | date or NULL | Closure date if closed | ISO date or NULL |
| closure_reason | string or NULL | Closure reason | customer_request, delinquency, inactivity, account_review |

Notes: DPD severity order: current < dpd_30 < dpd_60 < dpd_90 < dpd_120plus. For delinquency analysis always filter account_status IN ('active','delinquent').

### Group 4 — Behavioral Signals

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| months_since_last_purchase | integer | Months since last purchase | 0 to 48 |
| avg_monthly_spend_3m | float | Avg monthly spend last 3 months | 0 to 2000 |
| avg_monthly_spend_12m | float | Avg monthly spend last 12 months | 0 to 1500 |
| spend_trend_pct_3m | float | Spend change: last 3m vs prior 3m | -100 to +200 |
| total_spend_lifetime | float | Cumulative spend since origination | 0 to 80000 |
| login_frequency_30d | integer | Digital logins last 30 days | 0 to 19 |
| avg_login_frequency_3m | float | Avg monthly logins last 3 months | 0 to 19 |
| dormant_flag | 0/1 | 1 = no purchase in 3+ months | 0 or 1 |
| attrition_risk_flag | 0/1 | 1 = behavioral attrition pattern | 0 or 1 |

Notes: Strongest attrition signal = attrition_risk_flag=1 AND login_frequency_30d=0 AND avg_monthly_spend_3m < 20. spend_trend_pct_3m negative = falling spend.

### Group 5 — Risk Indicators

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| bureau_score_current | integer | Most recent bureau score | 300 to 850 |
| score_drift | integer | Current FICO minus booking FICO (negative = deterioration) | -200 to +50 |
| bureau_refresh_quarter | string | Quarter of last bureau refresh | 2024-Q4 |
| derogatory_count_current | integer | Total derogatory marks at snapshot | 0 to 8 |
| new_derogatory_flag | 0/1 | 1 = new derogatory in last refresh | 0 or 1 |
| utilization_stress_flag | 0/1 | 1 = utilization > 80% | 0 or 1 |
| hardship_flag | 0/1 | 1 = ever enrolled in hardship program | 0 or 1 |
| payment_plan_flag | 0/1 | 1 = ever on a payment plan | 0 or 1 |
| risk_segment | string | Derived risk classification | low_risk, standard, moderate_risk, high_risk, attrition_risk |

Notes: score_drift is pre-calculated (bureau_score_current - bureau_score_at_booking). Accounts with score_drift < -50 are high charge-off risk. risk_segment logic: low_risk = Superprime + current DPD; high_risk = Subprime/Nearprime + DPD 60+; attrition_risk = behavioral flag without delinquency.

### Group 6 — Performance

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| chargeoff_flag | 0/1 | 1 = account has been charged off | 0 or 1 |
| chargeoff_date | date or NULL | Date of charge-off | ISO date or NULL |
| total_payments_lifetime | float | Total payments received since origination | 0 to 100000 |

---

## Pre-Query Reasoning Framework

Before writing any query, work through these steps:

Step 1 — Grain: What is one row in the result? Account / Partner / Tier / Vintage?

Step 2 — Population scope: Default to active + delinquent accounts for portfolio health questions:
WHERE account_status IN ('active', 'delinquent')
Include charged_off or closed only when the question is about losses or lifetime performance.

Step 3 — Point-in-time vs cohort:
- Point-in-time: all rows are already December 2024 — just filter and aggregate
- Cohort/vintage: filter on vintage_cohort, compare metrics across groups
- Lifecycle stage: filter on months_on_book ranges (e.g., early = 1-12, mature = 25-48)

Step 4 — Delinquency rate:
SELECT partner_program_code,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN current_dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_30','dpd_60','dpd_90','dpd_120plus') THEN 1 END)
        * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS delinquency_rate_pct,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN current_dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_90','dpd_120plus') THEN 1 END)
        * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS serious_delinquency_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot
WHERE account_status IN ('active','delinquent')
GROUP BY partner_program_code ORDER BY delinquency_rate_pct DESC

Step 5 — Attrition pattern:
SELECT credit_tier_at_booking, COUNT(*) AS at_risk_accounts
FROM account_snapshot
WHERE attrition_risk_flag = 1 AND login_frequency_30d < 3
  AND avg_monthly_spend_3m < 20 AND account_status = 'active'
GROUP BY credit_tier_at_booking

Step 6 — Score drift:
SELECT partner_program_code,
    AVG(score_drift) AS avg_drift,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN score_drift < -50 THEN 1 END) AS severe_drift_accounts
FROM account_snapshot
WHERE account_status IN ('active','delinquent')
GROUP BY partner_program_code ORDER BY avg_drift ASC

Step 7 — Vintage comparison:
SELECT vintage_cohort, COUNT(*) AS accounts,
    ROUND(AVG(bureau_score_at_booking),0) AS avg_fico_at_booking,
    ROUND(AVG(score_drift),0) AS avg_score_drift,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN chargeoff_flag=1 THEN 1 END)*100.0/COUNT(*) AS chargeoff_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot
GROUP BY vintage_cohort ORDER BY vintage_cohort

---

## Business Definitions

| Term | Column / calculation |
|---|---|
| Active account | account_status = 'active' |
| Delinquent | account_status = 'delinquent' OR current_dpd_bucket != 'current' |
| Serious delinquency | current_dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_90','dpd_120plus') |
| Charged off | chargeoff_flag = 1 |
| Dormant | dormant_flag = 1 |
| Attrition risk | attrition_risk_flag = 1 |
| Score drift | score_drift (pre-calculated, negative = deterioration) |
| Utilization stress | utilization_stress_flag = 1 (>80%) |
| Vintage | vintage_cohort (origination quarter) |

---

## What this table CAN answer
- Current delinquency rate by partner, tier, channel, or vintage
- FICO score at booking vs current — drift analysis
- Dormant and attrition-risk account identification
- Charge-off rate by any segment
- Origination quality comparison across channels and vintages
- Utilization stress and risk segment distribution
- Lifetime spend and payment behavior by segment

## What this table CANNOT answer (state clearly, then offer proxy)
- Month-over-month roll rates — data is point-in-time snapshot
- Intra-month transaction detail — spend is pre-aggregated
- Granular bureau attribute history — only booking-time and current scores available
- Recovery amounts on charged-off accounts — not in this table
- Individual servicing interaction records — summarized as flags only

When a user asks for something outside scope, say: "Roll rate analysis isn't available in this snapshot — but I can show the distribution of accounts across DPD buckets, which gives a static view of the delinquency stack."

---

## Warning Thresholds (flag proactively with emoji)

| Metric | Warning | Critical |
|---|---|---|
| Delinquency rate 30+ | > 8% | > 15% |
| Serious delinquency 90+ | > 3% | > 6% |
| Charge-off rate | > 4% | > 7% |
| Utilization stress | > 25% of accounts | > 40% |
| Avg score drift | < -30 pts | < -60 pts |
| Attrition risk rate | > 10% of active accounts | > 20% |
| Dormancy rate | > 15% of active accounts | > 30% |

---

## Response Rules
1. Lead with headline finding before any numbers
2. Show metric + threshold context: "11.2% — above the 8% warning threshold"
3. Round: percentages to 1 decimal, dollars to 2 decimals or M/B, counts to integers
4. Flag threshold breaches with the emoji labels above
5. Acknowledge limitations explicitly when asked something out of scope
6. Suggest one follow-up question after every answer
questions: What is the current delinquency rate across the portfolio? Break it down by credit tier and flag anything above the warning threshold.
How does origination quality differ across vintage cohorts from 2021 to 2024? Show average FICO at booking and current charge-off rate per vintage.
Find all active accounts showing attrition signals — zero logins in the last 30 days, declining spend, and dormancy flag. Which partners are most exposed?
Which accounts have experienced score drift worse than -50 points since booking and are currently in a delinquent DPD bucket? Show the total balance at risk.
Compare the Furniture vertical against the Retail vertical on delinquency rate, charge-off rate, and average score drift. Which vertical presents more systemic risk?

demos.title: NBA
demos.body: A database with information about basketball matches from the National Basketball Association. Lists Players, Teams, and matches with action counts for each player.
questions: What is the average points scored by each player in the dataset?
Which team has the highest total rebounds in a specific game?
How many assists did each player make in games where they played more than 30 minutes?
What is the shooting percentage (field goals made/field goal attempts) for each player in the dataset?
Which player has the highest plus-minus rating in a specific game?

demos.title: CraftBeer
demos.body: Craft beers labeled by styles and composition. A separate dataset lists breweries by state.
questions: What is the average ABV (Alcohol by Volume) of beers produced by each brewery?
Which brewery has the highest IBU (International Bitterness Units) beer, and what is the name of that beer?
List all beers that have an ABV greater than 8% and their corresponding breweries.
How many different beer styles are produced by breweries in a specific state (e.g., California)?
What are the top 5 breweries by the number of beers they produce, along with the count of their beers?

demos.title: Atherosclerosis
demos.body: Atherosclerosis is a longitudinal 20 years lasting primary preventive study of middle-aged men.
demos.context: The dataset contains data on individuals who participated in the Atherosclerosis study. It includes information on their demographics, medical history, and various health-related measurements over a 20-year period.
questions: What is the average weight (VAHA) of individuals with diabetes (DIABET)?
How many individuals have a history of hypertension (HYPERT) and also smoke (KOURENI)?
What are the most common causes of death (PRICUMR) recorded in the Death table?
What is the trend of cholesterol levels (CHLST) over the years for individuals with a specific condition (NEMOC1) in the Letter table?
How does the average systolic blood pressure (SYST1) compare between individuals with and without a history of heart disease (SRDCE)?

demos.title: Card Transactions
demos.body: Card transactions data with details on issuer country, transaction amount, and other details.
questions: What is the total amount of transactions for each issuer country?
How many transactions were declined, and what are the most common decline reason codes?
What percentage of transactions were flagged as fraud, and how does this vary by industry segment?
Which channel has the highest number of successful transactions, and what is the average transaction amount for that channel?
What is the distribution of transaction types (e.g., purchase, refund) across different issuer regions?

demos.title: HR: Employee Data
demos.body: Employee master data with demographics, job details, hierarchy, salary, performance, and other details.
questions: Which departments have the highest average salary and how many employees work in each?
What is the average time between hire and termination?
Which department has the highest performance, i.e. with a performance rating of 4 or above?
Which departments has the best gender balance and how many male & female employees work in each?
Who are the employees hired most recently, and what are their job titles?

demos.title: Marvel Powers
demos.body: Every Marvel character and variant along with their powers (at their weakest and strongest) from Marvel Fandom.
questions: List the powers of the top 10 characters by total power
What is the character with the highest maximum strength?
Which characters are the smartest?
How many characters have a minimum durability score greater than 50?
What is the average maximum speed of all characters listed?
Which character has the widest range between minimum and maximum energy projection?

demos.title: EHR Data
demos.body: Electronic Health Records covering patient demographics, clinical metrics, comorbidities, medications, etc. over time
questions: What is the average age of patients in the dataset?
Which comorbidity is the most common among patients?
What is the distribution of medication prescriptions by category?
How many unique patients are recorded in the dataset?
What is the trend of patient visits over time?
Which clinical metric shows the highest variance among patients?
How many patients have been prescribed more than five different medications?
What is the correlation between BMI and blood pressure levels?
Which age group has the highest incidence of diabetes?
How many patients have been admitted more than once within a year?
